name: Istanbul
description:
Istanbul, the transcontinental city straddling Europe and Asia across the Bosphorus Strait, is one of the world's most historically significant and culturally rich cities. For over 1,600 years it served as the capital of two of history's greatest empires — the Byzantine and the Ottoman — and its layered history is visible in every neighborhood.

The Hagia Sophia, built in 537 AD as a Christian cathedral, later converted to a mosque, and now a museum-mosque, is one of the greatest architectural achievements in human history. Its massive dome, spanning 31 meters, seemed to float above the interior when it was built, and it remained the world's largest cathedral for nearly a thousand years. Across the square, the Blue Mosque (Sultan Ahmed Mosque) features six minarets and over 20,000 hand-painted blue Iznik tiles that give it its name.

The Grand Bazaar, one of the oldest and largest covered markets in the world, contains over 4,000 shops spread across 61 covered streets. Here you can find everything from hand-woven carpets and lanterns to spices, jewelry, and ceramics. Nearby, the Spice Bazaar (Egyptian Bazaar) fills the air with the aromas of saffron, cumin, dried fruits, and Turkish delight.

Istanbul's food scene is legendary, drawing from centuries of Ottoman culinary tradition and Mediterranean influences. Street food is a way of life — vendors sell simit (sesame-covered bread rings), balik ekmek (grilled fish sandwiches) by the Galata Bridge, and roasted chestnuts on every corner. Sit-down meals feature dishes like kebabs, meze platters, pide (Turkish flatbread), and lahmacun. Turkish breakfast is an elaborate affair with dozens of small plates including cheeses, olives, honey, eggs, and fresh bread.

A cruise along the Bosphorus offers spectacular views of Ottoman palaces, waterfront mansions (yalis), medieval fortresses, and the dramatic meeting point of Europe and Asia.
best_time: April to May, September to November
fun_fact: Istanbul is the only city in the world that spans two continents — Europe and Asia.
